seo_title: Sterling Silver Jewelry: Care, Properties & History | Moon Raven Designs
meta_description: Discover sterling silver jewelry at Moon Raven Designs. Learn about its composition, tarnish, hypoallergenic properties, and care tips for lasting beauty.
intro: Sterling silver is the quiet poet of metals—bright, responsive, and deeply human. At Moon Raven Designs, we work in 925 sterling silver (92.5% pure silver alloyed with 7.5% copper) because it holds both strength and softness. It catches light like morning on still water, yet it warms to the body's touch, recording the story of its wearing in subtle patina.
properties: Sterling silver is a living metal. Its bright white luster comes from the pure silver content, but the copper alloy gives it the durability needed for daily wear. Over time, exposure to air and moisture causes tarnish—a thin layer of silver sulfide that darkens the surface. This is not damage; it is the metal's natural dialogue with the world. For most, sterling silver is hypoallergenic, though the copper content can cause reactions in those with extreme copper sensitivity. It is softer than steel or gold, meaning it can bend or scratch under force, but it can be repaired and repolished indefinitely. The weight is substantial without being heavy—a piece that feels present on the skin.
care: To keep sterling silver bright, wear it often: the friction of skin and cloth polishes it naturally. When not worn, store in a tarnish-resistant pouch or a box with anti-tarnish strips. Avoid exposing it to chlorine, salt water, sulfur-rich foods (eggs, onions), and harsh household cleaners. To remove tarnish, use a silver polishing cloth gently—never a paper towel, which can scratch. For deeper cleaning, a warm water bath with mild soap and a soft brush works well. Rinse and dry thoroughly. If you prefer an oxidized patina, let tarnish develop naturally or use a liver of sulfur solution. For repairs or professional polishing, bring your piece to a jeweler.
who_chooses: Those drawn to sterling silver often value the marriage of everyday wearability and quiet beauty. It suits minimalists who want a single ring to hold a decade of meaning, and storytellers who layer chains like chapters. It carries the intent of permanence without pretense—a metal that ages gracefully, like a well-loved book.

faq.a: Yes. Sterling silver is real silver, but it is an alloy: 92.5% pure silver mixed with 7.5% other metals (usually copper) for strength. Pure silver is too soft for most jewelry, so sterling is the standard for fine silver pieces.

No, tarnish is not permanent. It is a surface reaction that can be removed with a polishing cloth or gentle cleaning. However, if left unchecked for many years, tarnish can become more stubborn, but it can still be professionally restored.

faq.q: Can you wear sterling silver every day?
faq.a: Yes, sterling silver is durable enough for daily wear. Many people wear their sterling rings, necklaces, and bracelets constantly. However, remove it during heavy labor, swimming in chlorinated water, or when applying lotions to minimize scratches and tarnish.

faq.q: Does sterling silver turn your skin green?
faq.a: Sometimes. The copper in sterling silver can oxidize and leave a greenish mark on skin, especially if you have acidic sweat or wear the piece in humid conditions. This is harmless and washes off easily. It is not a sign of poor quality.

faq.q: How can you tell if sterling silver is real?
faq.a: Look for a hallmark: '925,' 'Sterling,' or 'S925' stamped on the piece. You can also perform a magnet test—silver is not magnetic. A more reliable method is to take it to a jeweler for an acid test or XRF analysis.

faq.q: Is sterling silver better than white gold?
faq.a: It depends on your priorities. Sterling silver is more affordable and has a brighter white luster, but it tarnishes and is softer. White gold is more durable and hypoallergenic (if nickel-free), but it requires rhodium plating to maintain its color and is more expensive.
